objective: Document the selective work_products gitignore policy, progress, validation, and next steps for tomorrow.
read_only_context: git ls-files -o --exclude-standard work_products | xargs -r du -h | sort -h | tail -40
git check-ignore -v representative work_products docs, summaries, generated PDFs, and raw sampled CSVs

validation.result: 8117 files observed before targeted gitignore filtering.
about 40G; tree includes heavy reconstructed solver and generated render payloads.
5830 visible untracked files after selective filtering.

validation.command: git ls-files -o --exclude-standard work_products | xargs -r du -h | sort -h | tail -40
validation.result: largest visible files were mostly 1.3M to 5.2M SVG/CSV/PPTX artifacts after generated PDF fan-outs were ignored.

README.md, summary.json, and small CSV summaries are visible; generated PDFs, raw sampled field CSV dumps, sampled pressure detail rows, and trusted-wall wallHeatFlux detail rows are ignored by targeted rules.

next_steps: Review .gitignore as a standalone cleanup/documentation commit candidate.
Inspect visible work_products packages before staging any package content.
Use git check-ignore -v for any surprising visible or hidden artifact.
Add narrow generator-specific ignore rules if new large generated families appear.
